skill.name: jira-automation
skill.description: Automate Jira tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): issues, projects, sprints, boards, comments, users. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Jira tasks via Rube MCP including issue management, project operations, sprint workflows, and user management.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 72 static findings are false positives. The skill is a markdown documentation file (SKILL.md) containing tool descriptions and parameters for Jira automation via Rube MCP. The detected patterns (backtick execution, URLs, cryptographic algorithms) are markdown formatting syntax and legitimate setup references, not executable code or security vulnerabilities.

content.value_statement: Automate Jira issue creation, project management, sprint workflows, and team collaboration directly from Claude using Rube MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: Search and filter Jira issues using JQL queries
Create, edit, and assign issues to users
Manage agile boards, sprints, and backlogs
Add and list comments on issues with rich text formatting
List projects, find users, and manage project roles
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server to be configured and connected
Jira connection must be ACTIVE before running workflows
Custom fields require exact field IDs from JIRA_GET_FIELDS
User operations require account IDs, not email addresses

use_cases.title: Daily Standup Preparation
use_cases.description: Automatically fetch sprint issues, identify blockers, and summarize team progress for daily standups.

use_cases.title: Issue Triage and Creation
use_cases.description: Quickly search existing issues, create new bugs or tasks, and assign them to appropriate team members.

use_cases.title: Sprint Planning Assistance
use_cases.description: List boards, sprints, and backlog items to help plan sprint assignments and capacity.

prompt_templates.title: Find My Issues
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Jira for all issues assigned to me in project [PROJECT_KEY] that are in progress. Show the issue keys, summaries, and priority.

prompt_templates.title: Create Bug Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new bug in project [PROJECT_KEY] with title '[BUG_TITLE]' and description '[BUG_DESCRIPTION]'. Assign it to [ACCOUNT_ID] and set priority to High.

prompt_templates.title: Sprint Status
prompt_templates.prompt: List all active sprints for board [BOARD_ID]. Show sprint name, status, start date, and end date. Also list issues in each sprint.

prompt_templates.title: Add Contextual Comment
prompt_templates.prompt: Add a comment to issue [ISSUE_KEY] with the following: '[COMMENT_BODY]'. Use Atlassian Document Format for any formatting.

output_examples.input: Search for my in-progress issues in project PROJ
output_examples.output:
Found 3 issues:

1. PROJ-123 - Fix login button alignment
   Priority: High | Status: In Progress

2. PROJ-127 - Update API documentation
   Priority: Medium | Status: In Progress

3. PROJ-131 - Review pull request #45
   Priority: High | Status: In Progress

output_examples.input: Create a new task in project PROJ for fixing the dashboard loading issue
output_examples.output:
Successfully created PROJ-142:
- Type: Task
- Title: Fix dashboard loading issue
- Status: Open
- Project: PROJ

You can view it at: https://your-domain.atlassian.net/browse/PROJ-142

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas as they may change
Verify Jira connection status is ACTIVE before running any workflows
Use JIRA_GET_FIELDS to discover exact field IDs for custom fields
Check pagination parameters when working with large result sets
anti_patterns: Assuming field names are consistent across projects without checking JIRA_GET_FIELDS
Using display names or email addresses instead of account IDs for user operations
Skipping the connection verification step and failing mid-workflow
Ignoring pagination when fetching large lists of issues or projects

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server by Composio that provides integration with various tools including Jira. It enables Claude to interact with Jira through standardized tool calls.

faq.question: Do I need Jira API keys?
faq.answer: No. Rube MCP handles authentication through OAuth with your Jira account. You connect via the RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS tool which provides an OAuth flow.

faq.question: Why do I need to search for tools first?
faq.answer: Tool schemas can change as Rube MCP updates. Calling RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS ensures you have the current parameter definitions and tool names.

faq.question: Can I use this with Jira Server or Jira Data Center?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for Jira Cloud. Jira Server/Data Center may work but certain APIs and features may differ.

faq.question: How do I find the correct account ID for a user?
faq.answer: Use JIRA_FIND_USERS or JIRA_GET_ALL_USERS to search for users. The results include account IDs which are required for assignment and mention operations.

faq.question: What is JQL and do I need to learn it?
faq.answer: JQL is Jira Query Language for searching issues. Basic queries like 'project = PROJ AND status = "In Progress"' are covered in the skill documentation. You do not need to be an expert.
